fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 74 is inverted. Clamping is applied when `clamp` is False and skipped when `clamp` is True, which is the opposite of the intended behavior.

spec.expr: (clamp and y__3 >= min(y0, y1) and y__3 <= max(y0, y1)) or (not clamp and y__3 == y__1)

constraints.intent: The value after the conditional block should be clamped if and only if clamping was requested.
The code block that performs clamping should only be executed when clamping is enabled.

spec.expr: y__1 >= min(y0, y1) and y__1 <= max(y0, y1)

constraints.intent: If clamping is enabled but the clamping block is skipped, the value must have already been within the valid range.
The uninterpolated value y__1 should be within the y-bounds if and only if the interpolation parameter t is within [0, 1].
